steps.title: Identify intersections on the graph
steps.content: A solution to the system must be a point (x, y) where both curves pass through the same location. Reading the graph, the line and parabola intersect at two points: one in the second quadrant (upper left) and one in the first quadrant (lower right).

steps.title: Read coordinates from the grid
steps.content: At the left intersection point, the x-coordinate is −1 (one unit left of the origin) and the y-coordinate is 5 (five units up from the origin). Reading from the grid, this gives the point (−1, 5). This matches choice A — A is correct.
